vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry-defined specimens (P1-P4) are not visible as healthy, established plants. The desk surface contains only soil-filled containers and a white rabbit anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; bare soil.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; bare soil.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: Pothos foliage absent; white rabbit anchor remains.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; bare soil.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty. Soil appears dark and potentially waterlogged.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty. No signs of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Contains only the white rabbit anchor. No Pothos growth detected.
plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: Empty. No evidence of plant life.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no surface cracking observed.
biome_observations.debris: White rabbit anchor in P3; spoon present in early images, removed in later sequence; white particulate matter (likely perlite or mineral salt deposits) visible on soil surface.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Removal of the spoon from the P1/P2 vicinity.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_current: Static state. No growth, no decay, no movement. The biome is dormant or entirely deceased.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: Total systemic failure. The absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue suggests the plants have either been removed, died back completely, or were never successfully established in this substrate.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white rabbit anchor in a pot devoid of the registered Pothos (P3) is the primary anomaly. The lack of any green matter across all four pots is a significant deviation from the expected biome registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish a baseline by mapping the physical containers against the provided registry. Upon validation, it is clear that the biome is currently non-functional. There is no visible plant life in any of the pots. The soil appears consistently damp, but without biological occupants, this suggests a failure in the cultivation cycle. The white rabbit anchor remains as the only consistent landmark in an otherwise barren set of containers.
